overriding-and-specialization.label: Overriding and Specialization
overriding-and-specialization.summary: Keep one shared method name from CatalogItem, then override it in Book and Movie so each subclass can produce its own label.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.title: Override label() for a book
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.prompt: The base `CatalogItem` class already has `label()`. In `models/book.py`, override `label()` so a `Book("Project Hail Mary")` returns `Book: Project Hail Mary`. Use the provided `main.py` to print the result.
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.hint: Keep the method name the same as the base class.

help.concept: Overriding lets a subclass keep the same method name while changing the result.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.starterCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("Project Hail Mary")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("Project Hail Mary")
print(book.label())

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("Project Hail Mary")
print(book.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book

book = Book("Project Hail Mary")
print(book.label())

0.message: Book.label() should return the book-specific label.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.title: Override label() for a movie
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.prompt: Use the same base class idea with a different subclass. In `models/movie.py`, override `label()` so a `Movie("Inside Out")` returns `Movie: Inside Out`. Use the provided `main.py` to print the result.
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.hint: Use the same method name as the base class again.

help.concept: Different subclasses can override the same base method in different ways.
help.hint_1: The method name should still be `label()`.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.starterCode:
from models.movie import Movie

movie = Movie("Inside Out")
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.movie import Movie

movie = Movie("Inside Out")
print(movie.label())

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.solutionCode:
from models.movie import Movie

movie = Movie("Inside Out")
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.movie import Movie

movie = Movie("Inside Out")
print(movie.label())

0.message: Movie.label() should return the movie-specific label.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.title: Print both specialized labels
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.prompt: Keep the provided `Book` and `Movie` classes. In `main.py`, print the label for `Book("Coraline")` and the label for `Movie("Coco")`. This practice uses the same idea with different titles than the sketches.
try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.hint: Create one object of each subclass, then call `label()` on both.

help.concept: Shared method names make it easy to compare different subclass behavior.

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.starterCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("Coraline")
movie = Movie("Coco")
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("Coraline")
movie = Movie("Coco")
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

try_overriding_and_specialization_sketch.solutionCode:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("Coraline")
movie = Movie("Coco")
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

main_py.content:
from models.book import Book
from models.movie import Movie

book = Book("Coraline")
movie = Movie("Coco")
print(book.label())
print(movie.label())

sc-overriding-purpose.title: Choose the reason to override
sc-overriding-purpose.hint: Look for the choice about changing the result while keeping the method name.

help.concept: Overriding keeps a shared contract but allows subclass-specific behavior.
help.hint_1: The method name stays the same.
help.hint_2: The returned text becomes more specific.

options.a: To remove inheritance from the program
options.b: To rename the base method everywhere
options.c: To keep `label()` but return a book-specific result
options.d: To stop `Book` objects from having titles

fb-overriding-method-name.title: Fill in the overriding method name
fb-overriding-method-name.prompt: Complete the method name that both subclasses share.
fb-overriding-method-name.hint: Use the same method name from the base class.

help.concept: Overriding works when the subclass uses the same method name as the base class.
help.hint_1: The method returns the display text for the item.
help.hint_2: It is not the constructor name.

dr-overriding-workflow.title: Order the override steps
dr-overriding-workflow.prompt: Put the beginner workflow in order.
dr-overriding-workflow.hint: Begin with the base method, then specialize it in subclasses.

help.concept: It is easiest to override after the base behavior already exists.
help.hint_2: Printing the result comes after the subclass method is ready.

tokens.t2: Create `Book.label()` with a book-specific return value
tokens.t3: Create `Movie.label()` with a movie-specific return value
tokens.t4: Make objects and print both labels

sc-overriding-shared-contract.title: Keep the shared contract
sc-overriding-shared-contract.prompt: What stays the same when `Book` and `Movie` both override `label()`?
sc-overriding-shared-contract.hint: Think about what later code can still call on both objects.

help.concept: The shared contract is the method name that every subclass supports.
help.hint_1: The result can change, but the callable behavior stays aligned.
help.hint_2: Look for the answer about one shared method name.

options.a: Later code can still call `label()` on both subclasses.
options.b: Both subclasses must return the exact same string.
options.c: Neither subclass can add its own behavior anymore.
options.d: The base class stops mattering once one subclass overrides a method.

sketch-0.title: Start with one shared method name

sketch-1.title: A subclass can override the method
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
Overriding means a subclass keeps the same method name but changes the result.

```python
class Book(CatalogItem):
    def label(self):
        return f"Book: {self.title}"
```

The program can still call `label()`, but books now show a book-specific label.

sketch-2.title: Different subclasses can specialize the same contract
sketch-2.bodyMarkdown:
More than one subclass can override the same method in different ways.

```python
class Movie(CatalogItem):
    def label(self):
        return f"Movie: {self.title}"
```

`Book` and `Movie` keep the same method name, so later code can treat them the same way.
